chapter_title.ar: باب إِذَا الْتَوَى الَّذِي عَلَيْهِ الدَّيْنُ عَلَى الْغُرَمَاءِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 26 – When the one upon whom is the debt delays upon the creditors

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: موثق - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (19/55)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: موثق - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (19/55)
text: كَانَ أَمِيرُ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ( صلوات الله عليه ) يَحْبِسُ الرَّجُلَ إِذَا الْتَوَى عَلَى غُرَمَائِهِ ثُمَّ يَأْمُرُ فَيَقْسِمُ مَالَهُ بَيْنَهُمْ بِالْحِصَصِ فَإِنْ أَبَى بَاعَهُ فَيَقْسِمُ يَعْنِي مَالَهُ .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Ibn Fazzal,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘Amir Al-Momineen<sup>asws</sup> used to imprison the man when he delayed upon his creditors. Then he<sup>asws</sup> would order, so that his wealth would be distributed between them with the apportionment. So if he refused, then he<sup>asws</sup> would sell it, meaning his belongings’.
en.sarwar: 1. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from ibn Faddal from ‘Ammar who has said the following: “Abu ‘ Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said that ‘Amir al-Mu’minin, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, would keep in custody a procrastinating debtor; then command to distribute his assets among his creditors proportionate to their shares, and if he (the debtor) disagreed, he (the Imam) sold his assets, then distributed it among his creditors.’”
